title: Quiz: Quantum Approaches to Risk Analysis
description: Test your understanding of quantum algorithms for risk calculations

questions.question: What is the key advantage of Quantum Amplitude Estimation (QAE) for risk calculations?

options.text: It eliminates the need for Monte Carlo simulation
It provides quadratic speedup: O(1/ε) vs classical O(1/ε²)
It works without any quantum errors

questions.explanation: QAE achieves quadratic speedup over classical Monte Carlo. To achieve accuracy ε, classical MC needs O(1/ε²) samples while QAE needs only O(1/ε) quantum queries.

questions.question: How does classical Monte Carlo error scale with sample count N?
questions.explanation: Classical Monte Carlo error scales as σ/√N, where σ is the standard deviation. This means you need 4x more samples to halve the error.

questions.question: In quantum risk calculation, what is the purpose of the Grover operator?

options.text: To amplify amplitudes of states meeting certain criteria

questions.explanation: The Grover operator amplifies the amplitude of states marked by an oracle (e.g., states representing losses exceeding a threshold), which is essential for amplitude estimation.

questions.question: Why can't current NISQ devices achieve practical quantum advantage for VaR calculation?

options.text: High error rates and limited qubit count degrade the quadratic speedup

questions.explanation: NISQ devices have high error rates and limited qubits. The errors accumulate with circuit depth, and the required circuit depths for practical problems exceed current capabilities.

questions.question: Quantum Monte Carlo can provide exact results for risk calculations.
questions.explanation: False. Quantum Monte Carlo still produces probabilistic estimates, just with better scaling. The error decreases faster with resources (1/M vs 1/√N) but results are still approximate.

questions.question: How does quantum VaR estimation differ from classical VaR estimation?

options.text: It encodes scenarios as quantum amplitudes and uses QAE
It calculates a different risk metric
It only works for single assets

questions.explanation: Quantum VaR encodes loss scenarios as amplitudes in a quantum state, then uses quantum amplitude estimation to determine the probability of exceeding various loss thresholds, achieving quadratic speedup.

questions.question: What is the role of an oracle in quantum risk calculation?

options.text: To mark quantum states corresponding to losses above a threshold

questions.explanation: The oracle marks (flips the phase of) quantum states representing scenarios where losses exceed a threshold. This marking is then amplified by Grover iterations.

questions.question: Which risk calculations could potentially benefit from quantum computing? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Any risk calculation involving Monte Carlo simulation or searching over many scenarios can benefit from quantum speedup. Simple arithmetic operations don't need quantum acceleration.
